name: Portable skill system ceremony alignment
description: Restructure project-owned skill guidance into portable skill folders, make `using-skills` the single entry workflow router with mandatory initial triage, align core workflow skills around minimum-sufficient ceremony levels, clarify that every task requires initial thinking but not formal brainstorming, and remove stale skill-reference guidance without expanding metadata.

evidence.description: Project-level agent skills guide documents the portable folder-only skill structure, ownership rules, and `using-skills` entrypoint policy.
Shared ceremony definitions establish Level 0-3 workflow expectations and escalation rules for lightweight versus high-ceremony tasks.
Skill maintenance guidance replaces loose shared markdown with a portable discoverable skill and preserves the minimal-metadata policy.
The entrypoint skill now explicitly requires initial thinking for every task, owns initial triage, and routes to the minimum sufficient process instead of forcing high ceremony for simple tasks.
Brainstorming is now defined as deeper structured exploration that follows initial triage only when ambiguity, tradeoffs, or unclear direction remain.
Plan writing now starts once direction is clear and no longer implies that brainstorming must precede every plan.
Plan execution now escalates back to triage or brainstorming only when execution exposes invalid assumptions, ambiguity, or new tradeoffs.
Completion policy now requires fresh verification evidence for every ceremony level, including Level 0 direct tasks, with operational reporting instead of threat-heavy wording.
Code-review workflow now scales review expectations by ceremony level and only calls domain reviewers when warranted.
Progress log records both the original portable skill-system upgrade and this follow-up clarification about initial thinking versus formal brainstorming.
Full repository verification passed after the skill-doc and harness updates.
Final GitNexus change detection reported LOW risk with 9 changed files, 0 changed symbols, and 0 affected processes.
